title: Docker Control
description: Pilot the agentai stack from Open WebUI. Actions: ps, logs, restart <service>, up, pull, down. Requires admin role.
author: younes

fields.label: Docker-control API token
fields.help: Generated during install.sh. Stored in /opt/agentai/docker-control/.env on the VPS. Get it with: ssh prodserveur 'cat /opt/agentai/docker-control/.env'

content:
"""
title: Docker Control
description: Pilot the agentai stack from Open WebUI. Actions: ps, logs, restart, up, pull, down.
author: younes
version: 1.0.0
requirements: httpx
"""

import httpx
import json
from typing import Optional, List
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class Action:
    BASE_URL = "https://agentai.smartefp.com/api/control"
    ALLOWED_SERVICES = ["open-webui", "llm-proxy", "agentai-postgres", "agentai-redis"]


class Tools:
    class Valves(BaseModel):
        api_token: str = Field(
            "",
            description="Bearer token for docker-control API (see /opt/agentai/docker-control/.env on VPS)",
        )

    def __init__(self):
        self.valves = self.Valves()
        self.client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=180.0)

    async def _request(self, method: str, path: str) -> str:
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.valves.api_token}"}
        url = f"{Action.BASE_URL}{path}"
        try:
            r = await self.client.request(method, url, headers=headers)
            if r.status_code == 401:
                return "❌ Unauthorized: check the api_token in the tool config (Admin Panel → Tools → Docker Control)"
            if r.status_code == 403:
                return "❌ Forbidden: invalid token"
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                return f"❌ HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:500]}"
            return r.text
        except httpx.TimeoutException:
            return f"❌ Timeout calling {url}"
        except Exception as e:
            return f"❌ {type(e).__name__}: {e}"

    async def list_services(
        self,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        List all running Docker services in the agentai stack with their status.
        Use this to see what's currently running before restarting anything.
        """
        text = await self._request("GET", "/ps")
        if text.startswith("❌"):
            return text
        try:
            data = json.loads(text)
            lines = [f"**{len(data.get('services', []))} service(s) running:**\n"]
            for s in data.get("services", []):
                emoji = "✅" if "Up" in s.get("status", "") and "healthy" in s.get("status", "") else "⚠️"
                lines.append(f"- {emoji} **{s['name']}** : {s['status']}")
                if s.get("ports"):
                    lines.append(f"  Ports: `{s['ports']}`")
            return "\n".join(lines)
        except Exception as e:
            return f"Raw response: {text}\n(parse error: {e})"

    async def get_logs(
        self,
        service: str,
        tail: int = 50,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Fetch the last N log lines of a Docker service. Useful for debugging.
        :param service: One of open-webui, llm-proxy, agentai-postgres, agentai-redis, all
        :param tail: Number of log lines to retrieve (default 50, max 500)
        """
        if service not in Action.ALLOWED_SERVICES and service != "all":
            return f"❌ Unknown service '{service}'. Allowed: {Action.ALLOWED_SERVICES} or 'all'"
        tail = min(max(1, tail), 500)
        text = await self._request("GET", f"/logs/{service}?tail={tail}")
        # Truncate if too long for the chat context
        if len(text) > 8000:
            text = text[-8000:] + "\n\n... (truncated to last 8000 chars)"
        return f"**Logs of {service} (last {tail} lines):**\n```\n{text}\n```"

    async def restart_service(
        self,
        service: str,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Restart a single Docker service in the agentai stack.
        :param service: One of open-webui, llm-proxy, agentai-postgres, agentai-redis
        """
        if service not in Action.ALLOWED_SERVICES:
            return f"❌ Unknown service '{service}'. Allowed: {Action.ALLOWED_SERVICES}"
        text = await self._request("POST", f"/restart/{service}")
        return f"**Restart {service}:**\n```json\n{text}\n```"

    async def pull_and_deploy(
        self,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Pull the latest code from Git (git pull + docker compose pull + up -d).
        This can take 1-3 minutes. Use with caution.
        """
        text = await self._request("POST", "/pull")
        try:
            data = json.loads(text)
            parts = []
            for label, info in data.items():
                emoji = "✅" if info.get("rc") == 0 else "❌"
                parts.append(f"{emoji} **{label}** (rc={info.get('rc')}):")
                stdout = info.get("stdout", "").strip()
                stderr = info.get("stderr", "").strip()
                if stdout:
                    parts.append(f"  stdout:```\n{stdout[-1500:]}\n```")
                if stderr:
                    parts.append(f"  stderr:```\n{stderr[-500:]}\n```")
            return "**Pull + Deploy result:**\n\n" + "\n".join(parts)
        except Exception:
            return f"Raw response: {text}"

    async def bring_up(
        self,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Bring the entire stack up (docker compose up -d). Idempotent.
        """
        text = await self._request("POST", "/up")
        return f"**docker compose up -d:**\n```\n{text}\n```"

    async def bring_down(
        self,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Stop and remove the entire agentai stack. DESTRUCTIVE: containers will be
        removed but volumes are preserved. Use with caution.
        """
        text = await self._request("POST", "/down")
        return f"**docker compose down:**\n```\n{text}\n```"

    async def list_allowed_actions(
        self,
        __user__: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Return the list of services this tool can interact with.
        """
        return (
            "**Allowed services:**\n"
            + "\n".join(f"- `{s}`" for s in Action.ALLOWED_SERVICES)
            + "\n\n**Available actions:**\n"
            + "- list_services() — see what's running\n"
            + "- get_logs(service, tail=50) — fetch logs\n"
            + "- restart_service(service) — restart one service\n"
            + "- pull_and_deploy() — git pull + docker pull + up -d (1-3 min)\n"
            + "- bring_up() — docker compose up -d\n"
            + "- bring_down() — docker compose down (destructive)\n"
        )
